search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae abdominal pain adults investigations imaging referral red flags 2024
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic repeat imaging, laboratory test, referral, treatment, or urgency
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: The report and clinician interpretation must be entered explicitly.
No action follows solely from the generic workflow title.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Adults reviewing abdominal imaging obtained for a clinician-defined indication; modality, finding and clinical context are unspecified.
setting_applicability: Primary, gastroenterology, hepatology, imaging-result, or outpatient follow-up as qualified by the exact source.
UAE_applicability: UAE DHA evidence applies where cited; international gastroenterology and hepatology evidence requires UAE laboratory, imaging, referral, alcohol-service, prescribing, and local pathway adaptation.
recency_verification: Exact official sections were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: The cited official source remains current on the issuing organisation website at the review date.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact UAE adult section supports documenting the symptom context prompting result review, interval course, associated gastrointestinal, urinary or gynaecological symptoms, exposures and functional impact without interpreting the image.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Specifies onset, duration, location, character, severity, associated gastrointestinal, urinary, gynaecological, exposure, travel, trauma, and medical-history domains.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact section supports clinician-assessed severe pain, fever, vomiting, bleeding, weight loss, dysphagia, haematuria, pregnancy and shock context without asserting absence or urgency.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists sudden or severe pain, fever, vomiting, rigid abdomen, bleeding, weight loss, dysphagia, haematuria, pregnancy, and shock-associated features.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact section supports recording that an imaging report or laboratory result was already reviewed by the clinician; it does not interpret the finding or order further tests.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists blood tests, urinalysis, pregnancy testing, pancreatic enzymes, and imaging selected according to the clinical assessment.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact referral context supports documenting a clinician-entered plan, safety net and follow-up without generating referral or urgency.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists specialist and emergency referral criteria for adult abdominal pain.

unresolved_source_gaps: Result source and date, exact report text, incidental findings, comparison, clinician interpretation, diagnosis, action, and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
